steps.title: Account for sediment evidence
steps.content: Most of the sediment is fine mudstone, which forms by slow settling in low-flow water. Some fine grains show cracking from episodic drying, and the coarser sandstone intervals point to flowing water at times.

steps.title: Reject partial histories
steps.content: B overstates a stream-first sequence and makes the lake brief. C says the lake did not dry, contradicting the cracking evidence. D denies lake formation even though the mudstone evidence points to low-flow standing water. The answer is A.
